title: International Potential of Public Procurement Law
content:
## International Potential of Public Procurement Law
Please join us for an interactive discussion on this timely topic. Participation in procurement procedures is the perfect opportunity for Canadian companies to expand in the European market and vice versa. Public procurement represents a strategic entry point for firms looking to expand their international presence. Governments worldwide are increasingly seeking external expertise to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of public services, optimize resource allocation, and foster innovation within the public sector. This is the perfect opportunity for a wide range of business, from infrastructure, to information technology, to defence as well as a wide range of service providers across the economy to learn how to tap into this market. Speakers: • Robert Glasgow , Partner, McCarthy Tétrault, Toronto • Ljiljana Stanic , Associate, McCarthy Tétrault, Toronto • Dr. Christian Kokew , Partner, LUTZ | ABEL Rechtsanwalts PartG mbB, Germany Note: The presentation will take place as a webinar and will only be offered in English. For questions about this event, please contact clientevents@mccarthy.ca . This program is eligible for up to 1.5 Substantive Hours under the Law Society of Ontario’s mandatory CPD Regime.
